compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I can rely on enrich-file-impact.sh and sprint checks to use the structured file impact API
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor three consumer scripts to use the structured file impact API instead of ad-hoc markdown parsing:
1. enrich-file-impact.sh — LLM generation (Haiku API call) stays in the workflow layer; storage changes from ticket comment to `ticket set-file-impact`
2. sprint-drift-check.sh — read via `ticket get-file-impact` instead of parsing markdown
3. sprint-review-scope-check.sh — read via `ticket get-file-impact` instead of parsing markdown

**Why**: Eliminates the 4 ad-hoc markdown parsers that produce inconsistent extraction results. Single source of truth for file impact data.

**Scope**:
- IN: enrich-file-impact.sh refactor (storage swap to set-file-impact), sprint-drift-check.sh refactor (get-file-impact), sprint-review-scope-check.sh refactor (get-file-impact), existing test suites pass for all three scripts with no behavioral regression
- OUT: Event type (Story 2985-f04d); CLI (Story 3dad-e87f); Jira bridge (Stories C, D); migration (Story 54ee-c932)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, enrich-file-impact.sh stores file impact via `ticket set-file-impact` (not ticket comment); LLM generation remains in the workflow layer ← Satisfies: "enrich-file-impact.sh is refactored: LLM generation (Haiku API call) stays in the workflow layer; storage changes from ticket comment to ticket set-file-impact"
- When this story is complete, sprint-drift-check.sh reads via `ticket get-file-impact` (not ad-hoc markdown parsing) ← Satisfies: "sprint-drift-check.sh and sprint-review-scope-check.sh are also refactored to use ticket get-file-impact instead of ad-hoc markdown parsing"
- When this story is complete, sprint-review-scope-check.sh reads via `ticket get-file-impact` ← Satisfies: "all four ad-hoc parsers migrate to the structured API"
- When this story is complete, existing test suites for all three scripts pass with no behavioral regression
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all refactored logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Do NOT change enrich-file-impact.sh's LLM layer — only the storage layer swaps from ticket comment to ticket set-file-impact
- [Testing] Behavior-regression check: run each script's existing test suite before and after to confirm no regression
- [Maintainability] Sprint-next-batch.sh integration is deliberately NOT in this story — that is gated on Epic 2c28-e5b9's `ticket next-batch` subcommand (see epic dependency notes)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration: [Consumer Impact] sprint-drift-check.sh parses THREE markdown formats today (## File Impact bullet list, markdown table with |File|Action|Task(s)|, ## Files to Create/Modify). sprint-review-scope-check.sh parses the bullet list. When refactoring to use get-file-impact, keep the existing markdown parsers as a SECONDARY fallback path for tickets whose migration was skipped or failed (see migration story 54ee-c932 — unparseable tickets are logged and skipped, leaving them markdown-only). Recommended code path: try get-file-impact first; on empty result, fall through to the existing markdown parser. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Done definition update: Add issue-quality-check.sh (plugins/dso/scripts/issue-quality-check.sh) as a FOURTH consumer that must be refactored in this story. issue-quality-check.sh counts items in ## File Impact / ### Files to modify sections via awk regex (~lines 83-91) as part of the QUALITY gate used by ticket creation flows. Post-refactor, new tickets created post-migration will have zero markdown items and QUALITY will silently fail-over to the line-count/keyword legacy path — degrading the quality signal and potentially affecting downstream gating. Amend issue-quality-check.sh to also count get-file-impact array entries and treat non-zero as satisfying file_impact_items. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Done definition update: Update enrich-file-impact.sh idempotency check — the current code (lines 60-68) skips work when a ticket already has a ## File Impact section in its markdown. After the storage swap, this check MUST use get-file-impact: exit 0 when the returned array is non-empty; else run LLM enrichment + set-file-impact. Also update test-enrich-file-impact-v2-removal.sh if it asserts on the comment-path storage mechanism. Without this, re-invocations on post-refactor tickets call the LLM + set-file-impact repeatedly, doubling cost and producing duplicate FILE_IMPACT events (whose latest-wins semantics are defined in story 2985-f04d). [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
